title: I have a song I love to sing
firstLine: I have a song I love to sing,
publicDomainBasis: Edwin Othello Excell (1851–1921), Edwin Othello Excell (1851–1921). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
I have a song I love to sing,
Since I have been redeemed,
Of my Redeemer, Savior, King—
Since I have been redeemed.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
I have a Christ that satisfies,
Since I have been redeemed,
To do His will my highest prize—
Since I have been redeemed.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
I have a witness bright and clear,
Since I have been redeemed,
Dispelling every doubt and fear—
Since I have been redeemed.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
I have a joy I can’t express,
Since I have been redeemed,
All through the Lord, my righteousness—
Since I have been redeemed.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
I have a God who is for me,
Since I have been redeemed,
With whom I’ll dwell eternally—
Since I have been redeemed.
